title: Remote Graphic Designer Jobs in Canadian Gaming (2025)
description: Research and listings for remote graphic design and 2D art positions at Canadian game studios.

schemas.headline: Remote Graphic Designer Jobs in Canadian Gaming

mainEntity.name: Do Canadian game studios hire fully remote graphic designers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Since 2020, major studios like Behaviour Interactive, Phoenix Labs, and Klei Entertainment have maintained robust remote work policies. Many studios hire anywhere within Canada to tap into the nationwide talent pool, though some require you to reside in specific provinces (like Quebec or BC) for tax credit purposes.

mainEntity.name: What is the average salary for a remote graphic designer in the Canadian gaming industry?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Canada, a mid-level remote graphic designer in gaming typically earns between $60,000 and $85,000 CAD. Senior designers and specialized 2D artists can earn between $90,000 and $130,000 CAD, depending on the studio size and project scope.

mainEntity.name: What portfolio pieces do game studios look for in a graphic designer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Game studios look for highly polished marketing art, key art, logo design, typography tailored to specific game genres, UI/UX mockups, and social media assets. They want to see that you understand how to translate a game's core visual identity into compelling promotional materials.

mainEntity.name: Is French required for remote roles at Montreal-based studios?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends. If the studio is based in Quebec (like Ubisoft Montreal or Behaviour) and the role involves internal communication with local teams, bilingualism (French/English) is often required or strongly preferred. However, many studios hiring remotely across Canada operate primarily in English.
